blockingIssues.description: API key material is stored with SHA-256 (hashToken) instead of PBKDF2, violating required key hashing behavior.
Scope enforcement is incomplete: read/write scoped API keys are not consistently checked across authenticated endpoints (e.g., /spikes paths).
API key management write actions (DELETE/PATCH /auth/api-key/:key_id) do not enforce auth.scopes, allowing read-scoped keys to mutate key state.
`spikes auth create-key` overwrites the single stored auth token instead of storing API key credentials alongside the existing bearer token as required by the feature description.
Test isolation only overrides HOME and does not isolate XDG_CONFIG_HOME, so token lookup can still leak from real auth.toml on Linux environments.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit `followedProcedure=false` with a concrete deviation entry whenever implementation changes land before tests in a task that claims TDD-first flow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature api-key-backend transcript sequence shows schema/constants edits before `test/api-keys.test.ts` creation while handoff still marked followedProcedure=true.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify policy for handling known baseline test failures: if any tests are skipped, require deviation reporting and prohibit `followedProcedure=true` unless full-suite pass criteria were met.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature api-key-cli handoff reported `cargo test --jobs 5 -- --skip ...` and also marked followedProcedure=true despite skill text requiring all tests to pass.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit cross-platform auth-config isolation guidance for tests: set both HOME and XDG_CONFIG_HOME when validating auth.toml/token resolution behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fix-mcp-datasource-test-isolation addressed HOME only; reviewer found XDG_CONFIG_HOME can still leak host auth config.

rejectedObservations.observation: API-key hashing convention drift between shared guidance and implementation
rejectedObservations.reason: captured as a concrete blocking implementation defect; no additional shared-state update is needed before fix work
